meaning_english: To report or represent in outline; to foreshadow. Synonyms: Outline, Sketch, Intimate.
To sing or play a musical instrument very loudly. Synonyms: Shout, Roar.
To stop resisting or to collapse under pressure. Synonyms: Buckle, Yield, Accede.
To distribute something or to give criticism/punishment freely. Synonyms: Distribute, Inflict.
To use a resource or a quality as a source of help. Synonyms: Utilize, Employ, Exploit.
To defeat someone by a very small margin. Synonyms: Oust, Beat, Supplant.
To understand a difficult problem or person after much thought. Synonyms: Comprehend, Decipher.
To become expressionless or bored, often when listening to something dull. Synonyms: Go blank, Space out.
To talk or complain about something repeatedly and annoyingly. Synonyms: Dwell on, Reiterate.
To speak or write about something with great hostility. Synonyms: Fulminate, Rail, Denounce.
To drop or abandon something that is no longer wanted. Synonyms: Discard, Dump, Reject.
To cause a fuss, trouble, or a strong smell. Synonyms: Create, Provoke, Stimulate.
To accept something with enthusiasm (even if it is false praise). Synonyms: Relish, Devour.
To give a specific amount of something to someone. Synonyms: Apportion, Allocate.
To cancel or refuse to accept a plan. Synonyms: Veto, Cancel, Reject.
To talk more about your personal feelings or to create opportunities. Synonyms: Unburden, Reveal.
To gradually reduce the size or amount of something. Synonyms: Reduce, Trim, Diminish.
To become less noisy or silent. Synonyms: Hush, Calm.
To complain angrily about something. Synonyms: Fulminate, Inveigh, Protest.
To give someone an unpleasant responsibility or task. Synonyms: Burden, Encumber.
To become gradually smaller or weaker until it stops. Synonyms: Diminish, Wane, Decline.
To cause something new to start or to lead someone into a place. Synonyms: Herald, Introduce.
To guarantee that something is true or that someone is reliable. Synonyms: Guarantee, Attest.
To act as an attendant or to serve. Synonyms: Serve, Attend.
To cross something out with an X or to delete. Synonyms: Delete, Erase.
To give up possession of something or to reveal a secret. Synonyms: Relinquish, Surrender.
To stop paying attention to one's surroundings. Synonyms: Daydream, Space out.
To ask many people the same question. Synonyms: Inquire, Query.
To base your hopes or credit on something. Synonyms: Rely on, Depend.
To refuse to speak. Synonyms: Go silent, Mute.
